title: Make runs very, very slowly
body:
**Description**

In CI and Automation in general, it's often helpful to shallow-clone a repository to save time/space/bandwidth.  This is not practical with `https://github.com/containers/buildah.git` due to some unknown wizardry in the `Makefile`, causing `go` to spawn a billion (okay, maybe 20) `git` processes doing who-knows-what.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. On F33 with golang 1.15.14
2. `mkdir /tmp/go && export GOPATH=/tmp/go`
3. `git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/containers/buildah.git /tmp/buildah`
4. `cd /tmp/buildah`
5. `time make clean`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ time make clean
rm -f -r bin tests/testreport/testreport
make -C docs clean
make[1]: Entering directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'
rm -f buildah*.1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'

________________________________________________________
Executed in  347.31 secs    fish           external
   usr time  383.37 secs    0.52 millis  383.37 secs
   sys time   40.91 secs    1.20 millis   40.91 secs
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ cd /tmp
$ podman unshare rm -rf /tmp/go /tmp/buildah   # files are user-namespaced!?!?!?
$ git clone https://github.com/containers/buildah.git /tmp/buildah
$ cd buildah
$ git checkout 52dfdd7868b  # suspect next commit caused the problem
$ time make clean
rm -f -r bin tests/testreport/testreport
make -C docs clean
make[1]: Entering directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'
rm -f buildah*.1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'

________________________________________________________
Executed in  102.34 millis    fish           external
   usr time   59.73 millis    0.25 millis   59.48 millis
   sys time   50.80 millis    1.13 millis   49.67 millis
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

Not relevant

**Output of `buildah version`:**

Not relevant

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

Not relevant

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="33 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=33
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f33"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 33 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:33"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f33/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux fedora 5.13.12-100.fc33.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Aug 18 20:12:01 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

Not relevant

comments.body: I believe it is setting up a cache of all of the vendored content.  We don't see this slowness since we always comple against the cache.
Oh, that's probably the `go test -race pkg/rusage` call pulling down dependencies because we're not using `-mod=vendor` there.
See how much about go I know? :grin: Lemmie try my crude reproducer on your PR code...

...confirmed, that seems to fix it right up:

```
/tmp $ git clone --depth=1 file://$HOME/devel/buildah buildah
/tmp $ cd buildah
/tmp/buildah ((7e97cbdc…)|✔) $ time make clean
rm -f -r bin tests/testreport/testreport
make -C docs clean
make[1]: Entering directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'
rm -f buildah*.1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/tmp/buildah/docs'

________________________________________________________
Executed in  273.35 millis    fish           external
   usr time  322.16 millis    0.00 millis  322.16 millis
   sys time  122.84 millis    1.66 millis  121.18 millis
```

Thanks!
